content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (May 13, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on May 13, 2026, focused on diagnosing speculative decoding performance bottlenecks and hardware-specific inference issues. Efforts centered on identifying compatibility gates for SWA-based models and investigating bandwidth-efficiency cliffs on Intel Lunar Lake hardware.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS
*   **Speculative Decoding Diagnostics:**
    *   Identified a silent failure mode where speculative decoding is disabled for SWA-based models (e.g., Qwen3.6) due to partial sequence removal limitations.
    *   Documented the "bench-level signature" for this issue: absence of `n_drafted` counters in completion timings and a fallback to body-only inference throughput.
*   **Hardware Inference Investigation:**
    *   Confirmed a bandwidth-efficiency cliff on Intel Lunar Lake iGPUs for 14B-class models, where performance drops significantly below expected LPDDR5X bandwidth ceilings.
    *   Initiated a diagnostic matrix to test Vulkan queue and async toggles to isolate kernel-selection regressions.

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   None.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   None.

## NEW ISSUES
*   **Speculative Decoding Compatibility:**
    *   [#7635](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7635): Reported that speculative decoding silently disables `--spec-type dflash` for SWA-based bodies because the KV cache does not support partial sequence removal.
*   **Vulkan Performance Regression:**
    *   [#7638](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7638): Identified a bandwidth-efficiency cliff for `Qwen2.5-14B` on Lunar Lake iGPUs, running at only ~22% of the expected bandwidth ceiling.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   **Speculative Decoding (M-RoPE):**
    *   [#7631](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7631): Discussions focus on the M-RoPE position validation gate. Contributors identified that the harness's boundary-token re-feed pattern conflicts with strict monotone position requirements in `llama-batch.cpp`. A proposed fix involves rejecting incompatible M-RoPE pairs upfront to prevent silent performance degradation, while a deeper fix requires relaxing position checks.
*   **OpenVINO Integration (RFC):**
    *   [#7633](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7633): The RFC for adding OpenVINO as a runtime kernel is progressing. Benchmarks confirm that while NPU performance is poor for autoregressive LLM tasks, it provides a significant 1.8× speedup for Whisper ASR workloads. Consensus is forming to scope OpenVINO integration primarily for ASR and voice-agent pipelines rather than chat-generation.
